sigma_rules.plain_english: Detects when PowerShell scripts with the Romeo naming pattern are executed, which LeakNet ransomware uses to initiate the Deno runtime payload delivery process.
sigma_rules.rule:
title: LeakNet Ransomware Romeo PowerShell Script Execution
id: rule-0001
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of PowerShell scripts matching the Romeo*.ps1 naming pattern used by LeakNet ransomware to initiate Deno runtime process
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'Romeo'
    CommandLine|endswith: '.ps1'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate scripts with Romeo naming convention
level: high

sigma_rules.plain_english: Detects when VBScript files named Juliet are executed, which is the complementary script LeakNet uses alongside Romeo PowerShell scripts to launch their attack chain.
sigma_rules.rule:
title: LeakNet Ransomware Juliet VBScript Execution
id: rule-0002
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of VBScript files matching the Juliet*.vbs naming pattern used by LeakNet ransomware to initiate Deno runtime process
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'Juliet'
    CommandLine|endswith: '.vbs'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate VBScript files with Juliet naming
level: high

sigma_rules.plain_english: Detects when jli.dll is loaded from the USOShared directory, which is LeakNet's DLL sideloading technique to execute malicious code via legitimate Java processes.
sigma_rules.rule:
title: LeakNet Ransomware DLL Sideloading in USOShared Directory
id: rule-0003
status: experimental
description: Detects jli.dll sideloading from C:\ProgramData\USOShared directory used by LeakNet ransomware for persistence and execution
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|contains: '\USOShared\'
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\jli.dll'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, USOShared is not a standard Java location
level: critical

sigma_rules.plain_english: Detects when Deno runtime is used to execute base64-encoded or eval commands, which is how LeakNet loads its second-stage payload directly into memory to avoid file-based detection.
sigma_rules.rule:
title: LeakNet Ransomware Deno Runtime Malicious Execution
id: rule-0004
status: experimental
description: Detects Deno runtime executing suspicious commands including base64 decode or eval operations used by LeakNet ransomware for in-memory payload execution
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\deno.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'eval'
      - 'atob'
      - 'base64'
      - '--allow-all'
      - '--allow-net'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Deno development activities
level: high

sigma_rules.plain_english: Detects when the svhost.exe file is created in the user's Local Temp directory and then added to the Run registry key for persistence, which is a common LeakNet post-compromise behavior.
sigma_rules.rule:
title: LeakNet Ransomware Persistence via Fake svhost Executable
id: rule-0005
status: experimental
description: Detects creation and registry persistence of svhost.exe in AppData Local Temp directory used by LeakNet ransomware campaigns
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: registry_set
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\'
    TargetObject|endswith: '\svhost'
    Details|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\svhost.exe'
      - '\Local\Temp\svhost.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - None expected, svhost is a typo-squat of svchost
level: critical

snort_rules.plain_english: Detects HTTP traffic to the okobojirent domain which is a confirmed LeakNet command and control server used for second-stage payload delivery and victim communications.
snort_rules.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"MALWARE LeakNet Ransomware C2 Domain okobojirent"; flow:established,to_server; content:"Host|3a|"; nocase; content:"okobojirent"; fast_pattern; nocase; distance:0; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200001; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Major, created_at 2025-01-13, updated_at 2025-01-13, malware LeakNet;)

snort_rules.plain_english: Detects HTTP traffic to the mshealthmetrics domain which is a confirmed LeakNet C2 infrastructure used to coordinate ransomware operations and data exfiltration.
snort_rules.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"MALWARE LeakNet Ransomware C2 Domain mshealthmetrics"; flow:established,to_server; content:"Host|3a|"; nocase; content:"mshealthmetrics"; fast_pattern; nocase; distance:0; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200002; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Major, created_at 2025-01-13, updated_at 2025-01-13, malware LeakNet;)

snort_rules.plain_english: Detects any network connection to the known LeakNet C2 IP address used for command control and second-stage payload distribution during active ransomware campaigns.
snort_rules.rule: alert ip $HOME_NET any -> 185.234.217.99 any (msg:"MALWARE LeakNet Ransomware C2 IP 185.234.217.99"; flow:to_server; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200003; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Critical, created_at 2025-01-13, updated_at 2025-01-13, malware LeakNet;)

snort_rules.plain_english: Detects any network connection to the LeakNet staging server IP used for data exfiltration before encryption, which indicates an active breach in progress.
snort_rules.rule: alert ip $HOME_NET any -> 103.145.13.72 any (msg:"MALWARE LeakNet Ransomware Staging Server IP 103.145.13.72"; flow:to_server; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200004; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Critical, created_at 2025-01-13, updated_at 2025-01-13, malware LeakNet;)

snort_rules.plain_english: Detects HTTP requests containing Deno user-agent strings combined with base64-encoded content in POST requests, which matches LeakNet's method of retrieving encrypted payloads from C2 servers.
snort_rules.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"MALWARE LeakNet Ransomware Deno Runtime C2 Communication"; flow:established,to_server; content:"POST"; http_method; content:"Deno/"; http_user_agent; fast_pattern; content:"Content-Type|3a| application/"; http_header; pcre:"/[A-Za-z0-9+\/]{50,}/P"; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200005; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Major, created_at 2025-01-13, updated_at 2025-01-13, malware LeakNet;)

splunk.name: LeakNet Romeo/Juliet Script Execution
splunk.description: Detects PowerShell and VBScript files with Romeo/Juliet naming pattern used by LeakNet to initiate Deno runtime
splunk.query: index=* (sourcetype=WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational OR sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational) EventCode=1 (CommandLine="*Romeo*.ps1*" OR CommandLine="*Juliet*.vbs*" OR Image="*\\wscript.exe" OR Image="*\\powershell.exe") | eval script_pattern=case(match(CommandLine, "Romeo.*\.ps1"), "Romeo_PS1", match(CommandLine, "Juliet.*\.vbs"), "Juliet_VBS", 1=1, "Other") | stats count by Computer, User, CommandLine, ParentImage, script_pattern | where script_pattern IN ("Romeo_PS1", "Juliet_VBS")

splunk.name: LeakNet DLL Sideloading via USOShared
splunk.description: Detects jli.dll sideloading in C:\ProgramData\USOShared directory used by LeakNet for persistence
splunk.query: index=* (sourcetype=WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational OR sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational) (EventCode=1 OR EventCode=7) (CommandLine="*\\ProgramData\\USOShared*" OR ImageLoaded="*\\USOShared\\jli.dll" OR Image="*\\USOShared\\*") | stats count by Computer, User, Image, CommandLine, ImageLoaded, ParentImage | where match(ImageLoaded, "jli\.dll") OR match(CommandLine, "USOShared")

splunk.name: LeakNet C2 Communication and Staging
splunk.description: Detects network connections to known LeakNet C2 domains and IPs
splunk.query: index=* (sourcetype=stream:dns OR sourcetype=stream:http OR sourcetype=bro:conn OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational EventCode=3) (dest_ip="185.234.217.99" OR dest_ip="103.145.13.72" OR query="*okobojirent.com" OR query="*mshealthmetrics.com" OR url="*okobojirent.com*" OR url="*mshealthmetrics.com*") | stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, query, url, Computer | where isnotnull(dest_ip) OR isnotnull(query)

kql.name: LeakNet Romeo/Juliet Script and Deno Execution Chain
kql.description: Detects execution of Romeo PowerShell or Juliet VBScript files followed by Deno runtime execution
kql.query: DeviceProcessEvents | where (FileName in~ ('powershell.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'cscript.exe') and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ('Romeo', 'Juliet') and ProcessCommandLine has_any ('.ps1', '.vbs'))) or (FileName =~ 'deno.exe' and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ('powershell.exe', 'wscript.exe')) | extend ScriptType = case(ProcessCommandLine has 'Romeo' and ProcessCommandLine has '.ps1', 'Romeo_PowerShell', ProcessCommandLine has 'Juliet' and ProcessCommandLine has '.vbs', 'Juliet_VBScript', FileName =~ 'deno.exe', 'Deno_Runtime', 'Other') | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ScriptType | where ScriptType != 'Other'

kql.name: LeakNet USOShared DLL Sideloading and Registry Persistence
kql.description: Detects jli.dll loading from USOShared directory and svhost registry persistence mechanism
kql.query: union (DeviceFileEvents | where FolderPath has 'ProgramData\\USOShared' and FileName =~ 'jli.dll'), (DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine has 'USOShared' or FolderPath has 'USOShared'), (DeviceRegistryEvents | where RegistryKey has 'CurrentVersion\\Run\\svhost' or RegistryValueName =~ 'svhost') | extend EventCategory = case(ActionType has 'FileCreated', 'FileCreation', ActionType has 'ProcessCreated', 'ProcessExecution', ActionType has 'RegistryValueSet', 'RegistryPersistence', 'Other') | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, EventCategory

kql.name: LeakNet C2 Network Activity
kql.description: Detects network connections to known LeakNet C2 infrastructure including domains and IPs
kql.query: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteIP in ('185.234.217.99', '103.145.13.72') or RemoteUrl has_any ('okobojirent.com', 'mshealthmetrics.com') | extend ThreatIndicator = case(RemoteIP == '185.234.217.99', 'C2_Node', RemoteIP == '103.145.13.72', 'Staging_Server', RemoteUrl has 'okobojirent', 'C2_Domain_1', RemoteUrl has 'mshealthmetrics', 'C2_Domain_2', 'Unknown') | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ThreatIndicator | where ThreatIndicator != 'Unknown'

eql.name: LeakNet Script to Deno Execution Sequence
eql.description: Detects the execution sequence from Romeo/Juliet scripts to Deno runtime indicating LeakNet infection chain
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=5m [process where (event_simpleName="ProcessRollup2" and (FileName="powershell.exe" or FileName="wscript.exe") and (CommandLine="*Romeo*.ps1*" or CommandLine="*Juliet*.vbs*"))] [process where (event_simpleName="ProcessRollup2" and FileName="deno.exe")]

eql.name: LeakNet DLL Sideload and Java Execution in USOShared
eql.description: Detects jli.dll sideloading via Java process execution from USOShared directory
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=2m [file where (event_simpleName="DllLoadModule" and FilePath="*\\USOShared\\jli.dll")] [process where (event_simpleName="ProcessRollup2" and (FileName="java.exe" or FileName="javaw.exe") and CommandLine="*USOShared*")]

eql.name: LeakNet Persistence and C2 Communication Chain
eql.description: Detects registry persistence creation followed by network connections to known LeakNet C2 infrastructure
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=10m [registry where (event_simpleName="RegSetValue" and (RegObjectName="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\svhost" or RegValueName="svhost"))] [network where (event_simpleName="DnsRequest" and (DomainName="*okobojirent.com" or DomainName="*mshealthmetrics.com")) or (event_simpleName="NetworkConnectIP4" and (RemoteAddressIP4="185.234.217.99" or RemoteAddressIP4="103.145.13.72"))]

adversary.name: LeakNet Ransomware
adversary.description: LeakNet ransomware campaign leveraging ClickFix social engineering to trick users into executing malicious commands. The attack chain uses VBS and PowerShell scripts to deploy the Deno JavaScript runtime, which executes embedded payloads directly in memory while evading traditional detection mechanisms.

abilities.name: ClickFix Social Engineering Simulation
abilities.plain_english: Tests whether security awareness training and endpoint controls prevent users from copying and executing malicious commands from fake browser prompts - the ClickFix technique LeakNet uses to gain initial execution through user deception.
abilities.ability_yaml:
- id: leaknet-001
  name: ClickFix Social Engineering Simulation
  description: Simulates ClickFix social engineering technique where users are tricked into running commands from fake prompts
  tactic: execution
  technique:
    attack_id: T1204.001
    name: Malicious Link
  platforms:
    windows:
      psh:
        command: |
          Write-Host "[SIM] User clicked fake browser prompt"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Simulating ClickFix social engineering chain initiation"
          $simPath = "$env:TEMP\clickfix_sim.txt"
          "ClickFix simulation executed" | Out-File -FilePath $simPath
        cleanup: |
          Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\clickfix_sim.txt" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Write-Host "[SIM] Cleanup: Removed ClickFix simulation artifacts"

abilities.name: VBS Script Execution Chain
abilities.plain_english: Tests whether endpoint detection identifies Visual Basic scripts with suspiciously themed names launching execution chains - LeakNet uses Juliet-themed VBS files to initiate the PowerShell payload delivery stage.
abilities.ability_yaml:
- id: leaknet-002
  name: VBS Script Execution Chain
  description: Simulates Visual Basic script execution used by LeakNet (Juliet-themed VBS files)
  tactic: execution
  technique:
    attack_id: T1059.005
    name: Visual Basic
  platforms:
    windows:
      psh:
        command: |
          Write-Host "[SIM] Creating Juliet-themed VBS simulation"
          $vbsPath = "$env:TEMP\Juliet_sim.vbs"
          $vbsContent = @'
          ' Simulation VBS
          WScript.Echo "[SIM] Juliet VBS executed"
          '@
          $vbsContent | Out-File -FilePath $vbsPath -Encoding ASCII
          Write-Host "[SIM] VBS script chain simulated at: $vbsPath"
        cleanup: |
          Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\Juliet_sim.vbs" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Write-Host "[SIM] Cleanup: Removed VBS simulation file"

abilities.name: PowerShell to Deno Runtime Deployment
abilities.plain_english: Tests whether PowerShell script monitoring detects Romeo-themed scripts downloading and installing legitimate signed binaries like Deno runtime - LeakNet abuses this legitimate JavaScript runtime to bypass security controls.
abilities.ability_yaml:
- id: leaknet-003
  name: PowerShell to Deno Runtime Deployment
  description: Simulates PowerShell script (Romeo-themed) deploying Deno runtime as seen in LeakNet attacks
  tactic: execution
  technique:
    attack_id: T1059.001
    name: PowerShell
  platforms:
    windows:
      psh:
        command: |
          Write-Host "[SIM] Executing Romeo-themed PowerShell script"
          $psPath = "$env:TEMP\Romeo_sim.ps1"
          $psContent = @'
          Write-Host "[SIM] Romeo PowerShell executing"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Simulating Deno runtime download and installation"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Deno.exe would be placed in: $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Deno"
          '@
          $psContent | Out-File -FilePath $psPath
          Write-Host "[SIM] PowerShell deployment script created"
        cleanup: |
          Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\Romeo_sim.ps1" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Write-Host "[SIM] Cleanup: Removed PowerShell simulation script"

abilities.name: Deno Runtime Proxy Execution with Embedded Payload
abilities.plain_english: Tests whether security tools detect legitimate signed binaries like Deno being abused to decode and execute malicious JavaScript payloads directly in memory - LeakNet exploits this trusted runtime to bypass allowlists and minimize forensic artifacts.
abilities.ability_yaml:
- id: leaknet-004
  name: Deno Runtime Proxy Execution with Embedded Payload
  description: Simulates abuse of legitimate Deno runtime to execute embedded JavaScript payload in memory
  tactic: defense-evasion
  technique:
    attack_id: T1218
    name: System Binary Proxy Execution
  platforms:
    windows:
      psh:
        command: |
          Write-Host "[SIM] Simulating Deno runtime proxy execution"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Deno.exe (legitimate signed binary) bypassing blocklists"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Decoding embedded payload from Base64"
          $encodedPayload = "Y29uc29sZS5sb2coJ1tTSU1dIE1hbGljaW91cyBKYXZhU2NyaXB0IHBheWxvYWQnKTs="
          Write-Host "[SIM] Executing JavaScript directly in memory via Deno runtime"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Minimal forensic artifacts left behind"
          "Deno proxy execution simulated" | Out-File -FilePath "$env:TEMP\deno_sim.log"
        cleanup: |
          Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\deno_sim.log" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Write-Host "[SIM] Cleanup: Removed Deno execution simulation artifacts"

abilities.name: System Fingerprinting and Credential Discovery
abilities.plain_english: Tests whether security monitoring detects system reconnaissance commands including klist enumeration to discover Kerberos tickets - LeakNet fingerprints the victim host and enumerates credentials before establishing C2 communication.
abilities.ability_yaml:
- id: leaknet-005
  name: System Fingerprinting and Credential Discovery
  description: Simulates host fingerprinting and klist credential enumeration used by LeakNet
  tactic: discovery
  technique:
    attack_id: T1082
    name: System Information Discovery
  platforms:
    windows:
      psh:
        command: |
          Write-Host "[SIM] Fingerprinting host system"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Collecting system information for victim ID generation"
          $simInfo = @{
            ComputerName = $env:COMPUTERNAME
            UserName = $env:USERNAME
            OSVersion = [System.Environment]::OSVersion.VersionString
          }
          Write-Host "[SIM] System Info: $($simInfo | ConvertTo-Json -Compress)"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Executing klist enumeration for credential discovery"
          Write-Host "[SIM] klist.exe would enumerate Kerberos tickets"
          Write-Host "[SIM] Preparing to establish C2 connection with fingerprint data"
          $simInfo | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File -FilePath "$env:TEMP\fingerprint_sim.json"
        cleanup: |
          Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\fingerprint_sim.json" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
          Write-Host "[SIM] Cleanup: Removed fingerprinting simulation data"

campaigns.title: Nation-State Actors Exploit Notepad++ Supply Chain

iocs.context: C2 IP address observed in August incident with beacon traffic communication.
Second C2 server IP address used after initial C2, with communication lasting until September.
Download URL for malicious Lua script injection variant deploying Cobalt Strike beacon malware.
Malicious server IP address used for download attempts of Bluetooth DLL sideloading variant.
Download URL for Bluetooth DLL sideloading variant deploying Chrysalis backdoor.
Malicious NSIS installer often named update.exe that initiated complex infection chain.
Legitimate Bitdefender component misused to load malicious library log.dll that decrypted and executed custom backdoor.
Malicious library loaded via DLL sideloading that decrypted and executed custom Chrysalis backdoor.
Mutex known to be related to the Chrysalis backdoor malware.

ttps.description: Attackers compromised the official Notepad++ hosting infrastructure to serve malicious updates to targeted users
ttps.evidence: Between June and December 2025, the official hosting infrastructure for the text editor Notepad++ was compromised by a state-sponsored threat group known as Lotus Blossom. The attackers breached the shared hosting provider's environment. This allowed the attackers to intercept and redirect traffic destined for the Notepad++ update server.

ttps.description: Attackers used DLL sideloading with a legitimate Bitdefender component to load malicious library
ttps.evidence: This chain used DLL sideloading techniques and misused a legitimate Bitdefender component (BluetoothService.exe) to load a malicious library (log.dll) that decrypted and executed a custom backdoor.

ttps.description: Attackers used DLL sideloading with a legitimate Bitdefender component to load malicious library
ttps.evidence: We also observed a Bluetooth DLL sideloading variant in the same case. This Lua variant uses Bluetooth service DLL sideloading techniques to deploy the Chrysalis backdoor.

ttps.technique_name: Obfuscated Files or Information: Embedded Payloads
ttps.description: Malicious DLL decrypted and executed custom backdoor payload
ttps.evidence: DLL sideloading techniques and misused a legitimate Bitdefender component (BluetoothService.exe) to load a malicious library (log.dll) that decrypted and executed a custom backdoor.

ttps.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Cloud API
ttps.description: Attackers utilized Lua scripts for malicious code execution
ttps.evidence: In another infection chain, attackers utilized an NSIS installer to execute a command to run a malicious Lua script to load Cobalt Strike Beacon.

ttps.technique_name: Obfuscated Files or Information: Encrypted/Encoded File
ttps.description: Chrysalis malware employed custom API hashing to reduce antivirus detection
ttps.evidence: This malware, called Chrysalis, employed advanced evasion techniques. These included: Custom API hashing to reduce antivirus detection

ttps.description: Malware communicated with C2 servers over web protocols
ttps.evidence: In an August incident, we observed communication with a command-and-control (C2) IP address 45.76.155[.]202. After days of C2 beacon traffic to this IP address, attackers shifted to a second C2 server at 45.77.31[.]210

ttps.technique_name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
ttps.description: Malicious installer named to appear as legitimate update file
ttps.evidence: When targeted victims attempted to update their software, they downloaded a malicious NSIS installer. This installer — often named update.exe

ttps.description: Lua script injection used to inject shellcode and deliver Cobalt Strike
ttps.evidence: In September and October 2025, we observed a Lua script injection variant deploying malicious Lua scripts to inject shellcode. This attack used the EnumWindowStationsW API and resulted in the delivery of Cobalt Strike beacon malware.

ttps.description: Lua script injection used to inject shellcode and deliver Cobalt Strike
ttps.evidence: In September and October 2025, we observed a Lua script injection variant deploying malicious Lua scripts to inject shellcode. This attack used the EnumWindowStationsW API and resulted in the delivery of Cobalt Strike beacon malware.

ttps.description: Malware used mutex to ensure single instance execution
ttps.evidence: Identifies a Mutex known to be related to the chrysalis backdoor malware... filter mutex = 'Global\Jdhfv_1.0.1'

sigma_rules.rule:
title: Chrysalis Backdoor Mutex Detection
id: rule-0001
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of mutex associated with Chrysalis backdoor malware used by Lotus Blossom threat actor
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    - CommandLine|contains: 'Global\Jdhfv_1.0.1'
    - Image|endswith:
      - '\BluetoothService.exe'
      - '\update.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, mutex name is highly specific to this malware family
level: critical

title: DLL Sideloading via Bitdefender BluetoothService Component
id: rule-0002
status: experimental
description: Detects DLL sideloading attack using legitimate Bitdefender BluetoothService.exe to load malicious log.dll associated with Chrysalis backdoor
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\BluetoothService.exe'
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\log.dll'
  filter_legitimate:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Bitdefender\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Bitdefender\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legitimate
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Bitdefender installations with custom log.dll in proper directories
level: high

title: Suspicious Update.exe NSIS Installer Execution
id: rule-0003
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of update.exe NSIS installer associated with Notepad++ supply chain compromise by Lotus Blossom
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\update.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/S'
      - '/NCRC'
  filter_legitimate:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\notepad++.exe'
      - '\GUP.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legitimate
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software updates from trusted locations
  - Administrative software deployment
level: high

title: Lua Script Injection with EnumWindowStationsW API for Shellcode Execution
id: rule-0004
status: experimental
description: Detects Lua script injection variant using EnumWindowStationsW API to inject shellcode and deliver Cobalt Strike beacon
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_access
detection:
  selection:
    CallTrace|contains: 'EnumWindowStationsW'
    SourceImage|endswith:
      - '\lua.exe'
      - '\lua5.1.exe'
      - '\lua5.3.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Lua-based applications using window enumeration
  - Development and testing environments
level: high

title: Network Connection to Lotus Blossom C2 Infrastructure
id: rule-0005
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to known Lotus Blossom C2 servers associated with Chrysalis backdoor and Notepad++ supply chain compromise
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp:
      - '45.76.155.202'
      - '45.77.31.210'
      - '45.32.144.255'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - None expected, these are confirmed malicious infrastructure
level: critical

snort_rules.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> [45.76.155.202,45.77.31.210,45.32.144.255] any (msg:"MALWARE Lotus Blossom Chrysalis C2 Beacon Traffic"; flow:established,to_server; reference:url,unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/notepad-infrastructure-compromise/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200001; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity Critical, created_at 2025-01-21, performance_impact Low, affected_product Windows;)

alert http $HOME_NET any -> [45.76.155.202,45.32.144.255] any (msg:"MALWARE Lotus Blossom Malicious Update.exe Download Request"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"GET"; http.uri; content:"/update/update.exe"; fast_pattern; reference:url,unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/notepad-infrastructure-compromise/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200002; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity Critical, created_at 2025-01-21, performance_impact Low, affected_product Windows;)

alert http any any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"MALWARE Lotus Blossom Chrysalis Backdoor HTTP C2 Communication"; flow:established,to_client; http.header; content:"Content-Type"; nocase; pcre:"/Content-Type:\s*application\x2f(octet-stream|x-msdownload)/i"; reference:url,unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/notepad-infrastructure-compromise/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200003; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity High, created_at 2025-01-21, performance_impact Moderate, affected_product Windows;)

alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"MALWARE Potential Cobalt Strike Beacon via Lua Script Injection"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|00 00|"; depth:2; content:"|00 00|"; distance:2; within:2; threshold:type both, track by_src, count 5, seconds 60; reference:url,unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/notepad-infrastructure-compromise/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200004; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity High, created_at 2025-01-21, performance_impact Moderate, affected_product Windows;)

alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any 53 (msg:"MALWARE DNS Query to Lotus Blossom C2 Infrastructure"; dns.query; content:"45.76.155.202"; fast_pattern; nocase; reference:url,unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/notepad-infrastructure-compromise/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9200005; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity Critical, created_at 2025-01-21, performance_impact Low, affected_product Windows;)

splunk.name: Chrysalis C2 Communication Detection
splunk.description: Detects network connections to known Lotus Blossom C2 infrastructure

splunk.name: Malicious DLL Sideloading with BluetoothService
splunk.description: Identifies DLL sideloading attempts using legitimate Bitdefender BluetoothService.exe loading suspicious log.dll
splunk.query: index=* (EventCode=7 OR sourcetype=sysmon) (Image="*BluetoothService.exe*" OR ParentImage="*BluetoothService.exe*") (ImageLoaded="*log.dll*" OR CommandLine="*log.dll*") | stats count by ComputerName, Image, ImageLoaded, User

splunk.name: Chrysalis Mutex and Malicious Update.exe Detection
splunk.description: Hunts for Chrysalis backdoor mutex creation and suspicious update.exe execution patterns
splunk.query: index=* (EventCode IN (1,12,13) OR sourcetype=sysmon) ("Global\\Jdhfv_1.0.1" OR (Image="*update.exe*" AND (CommandLine="*lua*" OR CommandLine="*BluetoothService*"))) | stats count by ComputerName, Image, CommandLine, TargetObject

kql.name: Lotus Blossom Network Connections to C2 Infrastructure
kql.description: Detects outbound connections to known Chrysalis C2 servers
kql.query: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteIP in ('45.76.155.202', '45.77.31.210', '45.32.144.255') or RemoteUrl has_any ('45.76.155.202', '45.77.31.210', '45.32.144.255') | summarize ConnectionCount=count(), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteIP, RemotePort | where ConnectionCount > 3

kql.name: DLL Sideloading via BluetoothService.exe
kql.description: Identifies suspicious DLL loading patterns associated with Chrysalis backdoor deployment
kql.query: DeviceImageLoadEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ 'BluetoothService.exe' and FileName =~ 'log.dll' | join kind=inner (DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName =~ 'BluetoothService.exe' and FolderPath !startswith 'C:\\Program Files\\Bitdefender') on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath

kql.name: Suspicious Update.exe with Lua Script Execution
kql.description: Detects malicious NSIS installer named update.exe spawning Lua script processes or creating Chrysalis mutex
kql.query: DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName =~ 'update.exe' and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ('lua', 'BluetoothService', '.lua') or ProcessCommandLine has 'EnumWindowStationsW') | extend MutexCheck = (InitiatingProcessFileName has 'Global\\Jdhfv_1.0.1') | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, FolderPath

eql.name: Chrysalis DLL Sideloading Attack Chain
eql.description: Detects full DLL sideloading sequence from update.exe to BluetoothService.exe loading malicious log.dll
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=5m [process where event_id == 1 and process_name == "update.exe"] [process where event_id == 1 and process_name == "BluetoothService.exe" and parent_process_name == "update.exe"] [library where event_id == 7 and file_name == "log.dll" and process_name == "BluetoothService.exe"]

eql.name: Lua Script Injection and Cobalt Strike Beacon Deployment
eql.description: Identifies Lua script execution followed by suspicious network connections indicative of Cobalt Strike beacon
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=10m [process where event_id == 1 and (process_name == "update.exe" or command_line matches ".*\\.lua.*")] [network where event_id == 3 and (destination_address == "45.76.155.202" or destination_address == "45.77.31.210" or destination_address == "45.32.144.255")]

eql.name: Chrysalis Mutex Creation with C2 Communication
eql.description: Correlates Chrysalis backdoor mutex creation with subsequent C2 beacon traffic
eql.query: sequence by ComputerName with maxspan=15m [registry where event_id in (12, 13) and registry_path matches ".*Global\\\\Jdhfv_1\\.0\\.1.*"] [network where event_id == 3 and (destination_address in ("45.76.155.202", "45.77.31.210", "45.32.144.255") and destination_port in (80, 443, 8080))]

adversary.name: Lotus Blossom - Chrysalis
adversary.description: State-sponsored threat group Lotus Blossom compromised Notepad++ hosting infrastructure between June-December 2025 to deliver the Chrysalis backdoor and Cobalt Strike via malicious updates. Attack chains utilized DLL side-loading with legitimate signed binaries, Lua script injection, and advanced evasion techniques targeting software supply chain vulnerabilities.

abilities.name: Simulate DLL Side-Loading with Legitimate Binary
abilities.description: Simulates DLL side-loading technique using a legitimate appearing executable to load malicious DLL, mimicking Bitdefender BluetoothService.exe abuse
abilities.command: powershell.exe -Command "Write-Host '[SIM] Placing benign executable LegitimateService.exe'; New-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\LegitimateService.exe' -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null; Write-Host '[SIM] Creating sideload DLL log.dll in same directory'; New-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\log.dll' -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null; Write-Host '[SIM] DLL side-loading simulation complete - would load malicious payload'"
abilities.cleanup: powershell.exe -Command "Remove-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\LegitimateService.exe' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\log.dll' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host '[SIM] Cleanup: Removed simulated side-loading files'"

abilities.name: Simulate Malicious Software Update Delivery
abilities.description: Simulates masquerading as legitimate software update installer, mimicking compromised Notepad++ update mechanism
abilities.technique_name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
abilities.command: powershell.exe -Command "Write-Host '[SIM] Creating masqueraded update installer'; New-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\update.exe' -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null; Set-Content -Path '$env:TEMP\update.exe' -Value '[SIM] Malicious NSIS installer payload'; Write-Host '[SIM] Installer masquerading as legitimate software update'"
abilities.cleanup: powershell.exe -Command "Remove-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\update.exe' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host '[SIM] Cleanup: Removed masqueraded installer'"

abilities.name: Simulate Encrypted Payload Decryption
abilities.description: Simulates decryption of embedded encrypted payload using custom API hashing techniques for evasion
abilities.technique_name: Obfuscated Files or Information: Embedded Payloads
abilities.command: powershell.exe -Command "Write-Host '[SIM] Creating encrypted payload file'; $encPayload = 'U2ltdWxhdGVkRW5jcnlwdGVkUGF5bG9hZA=='; Set-Content -Path '$env:TEMP\encrypted_payload.dat' -Value $encPayload; Write-Host '[SIM] Simulating custom API hash-based decryption'; $decoded = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String($encPayload)); Write-Host '[SIM] Decrypted payload (would execute Chrysalis backdoor): $decoded'"
abilities.cleanup: powershell.exe -Command "Remove-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\encrypted_payload.dat' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host '[SIM] Cleanup: Removed encrypted payload file'"

abilities.name: Simulate Lua Script Execution
abilities.description: Simulates execution of malicious Lua script for shellcode injection, mimicking Cobalt Strike delivery mechanism
abilities.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Cloud API
abilities.command: powershell.exe -Command "Write-Host '[SIM] Creating malicious Lua script'; $luaScript = 'print(\"[SIM] Lua shellcode injection\")'; Set-Content -Path '$env:TEMP\malicious.lua' -Value $luaScript; Write-Host '[SIM] Executing Lua script via interpreter'; Write-Host '[SIM] EnumWindowStationsW API called for injection'; Write-Host '[SIM] Cobalt Strike beacon deployment simulated'"
abilities.cleanup: powershell.exe -Command "Remove-Item -Path '$env:TEMP\malicious.lua' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host '[SIM] Cleanup: Removed Lua script file'"

abilities.name: Simulate C2 Communication Over HTTP
abilities.description: Simulates command and control beacon traffic to remote server over web protocols
abilities.command: powershell.exe -Command "Write-Host '[SIM] Initiating C2 beacon communication'; Write-Host '[SIM] Connecting to C2 server 45.76.155[.]202 (simulated)'; Write-Host '[SIM] Sending HTTP POST beacon with system info'; Write-Host '[SIM] C2 response received - awaiting commands'; Write-Host '[SIM] Fallback C2 server 45.77.31[.]210 registered'"
